stories.title: Ukraine hits St. Petersburg site as Putin’s Davos kicks off
stories.summary: KYIV — Ukraine attacked an oil terminal in St. Petersburg in the early hours of Wednesday morning — just before Russian President Vladimir Putin’s big economic forum kicked off in the city. “Important facilities on Russian territory were hit last night. Among them was the Petersburg Oil Terminal. The distance from Ukraine is about 1,100 kilometers. Purely military targets at the Kronstadt base were also hit,” Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said in a statement Wednesday. The St. Pe

stories.title: Trump administration proposes 25% tariffs on Brazil despite US trade surplus
stories.summary: US claims world’s 10th-biggest economy engages in ‘unreasonable’ trade practices that ‘restrict US commerce’ Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Trump administration proposed 25% tariffs on imports from Brazil , charging that the world’s 10th-biggest economy engages in trade practices that are “unreasonable’’ and that “burden or restrict US commerce”. Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said he received the decision “with indignation”. The Brazil president also blamed the decision by the

stories.title: The EU should fast-track Ukraine’s membership of the club – it has the most to gain | Mujtaba Rahman
stories.summary: Many EU governments oppose it, but the strategic advantages of swift admission could be immense Russia’s war on Ukraine is now in its fifth year and a ceasefire remains elusive. The US’s attention is divided, limiting external pressure for compromise, while Moscow and Kyiv both still believe they can strengthen their respective negotiating positions through battlefield gains. At some point, however, a deal will have to be done. The parameters of that deal are already understood by negotiators on

stories.title: Three abandoned children, two missing parents and a 40-year mystery – podcast
stories.summary: We are raiding the Guardian long read archives to bring you some classic pieces from years past, with new introductions from the authors. This week, from 2023: Elvira and her brothers, Ricard and Ramón, were left at a train station in Barcelona aged two, four and five. As an adult, when Elvira decided to look for her parents, she discovered a family history wilder than anything she had imagined By Giles Tremlett. Read by Luis Soto Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany's Merz backs Hungary's new pro-EU course
stories.summary: After talks with Hungary's new prime minister, Peter Magyar, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz expects a positive reset in EU relations. Meanwhile, Budapest has signaled only a partial shift so far on support for Ukraine.

stories.title: Canada formally requests 16-year renewal of North American free trade pact
stories.summary: In a letter to his North American counterparts, US-Canada Trade Minister Dominic LeBlanc said the three countries should renew the USMCA trade deal.

stories.title: Canada calls for US and Mexico free trade deal to be renewed for 16 years
stories.summary: “The Agreement is highly beneficial to each of our countries and to the integrated North American economy," a letter conveying Canada’s recommendation, sent to the US and Mexico trade representatives, said.

stories.title: Alapjaiban kéne átírni a szabályokat, hogy vonzóbb legyen albérletben lakni Magyarországon
stories.summary: Számos élethelyzetben nem praktikus a magántulajdon túlsúlya a hazai lakáspiacon, amire megoldás lenne, ha nem csak magánszemélyek adnának ki lakásokat. Összeszedtük, hogy jelenleg mi akadályozza ezt.

stories.title: Orbán döntött arról, hogy le kell csapni az ukrán „aranykonvojra”, még a rajtaütés időpontja is a kormányzattól jött
stories.summary: Szakmailag semmi nem indokolta, hogy az ukrán pénzszállítókon rajtaüssenek, az ukáz a legmagasabb szintről jött: Orbán Viktor rendelte meg március elején – árulták el a Telexnek az „aranykonvoj” elleni művelet részleteit ismerő források.

stories.title: Aranykonvoj-ügy: Lemondásra szólítja fel a legfőbb ügyészt az ukránok ügyvédje
stories.summary: Az általunk megismert iratok és információk szerint az ukrán szállítmány elleni akciót feltehetően három szerv politikai megrendelésre készítette elő.

stories.title: Török Gábor szerint Orbán Viktor hatalmat gyakorolt, Magyar Péter politikát művel
stories.summary: Az elemző szerint nem lehet csak úgy elengedni a múltat.

stories.title: Megvan a MÁV szolgáltatócégének új vezérigazgatója, teljes szervezeti megújulást ígérnek
stories.summary: Az új MÁV SZK az átláthatóság, gazdaságosság és ügyfélközpontúság vállalata lesz, írja közleményében a MÁV-csoport.

stories.title: Magyar szerint Orbán kézi vezérléssel irányította a szervek munkáját, vállalnia kell a felelősséget
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök arra reagált, miszerint Orbán Viktor döntött arról, hogy le kell csapni az ukrán „aranykonvojra”.

stories.title: Orbán Anita az első, Schmidt Mária és Lévai Anikó lecsúszott – kijött a Forbes legbefolyásosabb magyar nők listája
stories.summary: Orbán Ráhel is eltűnt a listáról, a Tisza-kormány miniszterei az elsők között.

stories.title: Egyéni szinten lehetnek fájdalmasak a választás miatt elnapolt, hamarosan élesedő díjemelések
stories.summary: Bizonyos szolgáltatások akár 10 százalék felett is drágulhatnak, de összességében az inflációt ez csak alig észrevehető mértékben fogja befolyásolni.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor Navracsics Tibort is aktiválta a Fidesz megújulása jegyében
stories.summary: Megalakult a Védvonal, lesz biztonságpolitikai műhely, önkormányzati kabinet és Európa Klub is.

stories.title: Pósfai Gábor felmentette a budapesti rendőrfőkapitányt, és ki is nevezte az utódját
stories.summary: Terdik Tamás 2018 óta volt budapesti rendőrfőkapitány, utódja Baricska Norbert Tamás, az eddigi bűnügyi helyettes.

stories.title: Méretes veszteséggel zártak tavaly az itthon működő akkugyárak
stories.summary: A friss riportok szerint a 2024-es évhez képest 2025-ben csökkenő bevételek és növekvő veszteségek jellemezték az akkuágazat magyar cégeit.

stories.title: Annyira hatékonynak tűnik a hasnyálmirigyrák kezelésében egy új gyógyszer, hogy állva tapsolták meg egy onkológiai konferencián
stories.summary: A daraxonrasib hatóanyagú tabletta megduplázta az egyik legnehezebben kezelhető ráktípusban szenvedő betegek túlélési idejét.

stories.title: Ahol minden a szépségről szól: ilyen volt az idei Beauty Expo
stories.summary: Május utolsó hétvégéjén, az MVM Dome-ban rendezték meg a Rossmann és a Krémmánia szervezésében az ötödik Beauty Expót. Magyarország egyik legnagyobb szépségápolási eseménye két napon át kínált interaktív programokat, szakértői előadásokat és exkluzív termékbemutatókat a látogatóknak. Ahogy a korábbi években, úgy idén is forgattunk az eseményen.

stories.title: Magyar Péter Párizsban koszorúzott, délután Emmanuel Macronnal találkozik
stories.summary: 13 órakor találkoznak az Élysée Palotában a francia elnökkel, majd később a Nemzetgyűlés elnökével is tárgyal.

stories.title: EBRD- és OECD-előrejelzések: Magyarország az uniós forrásokkal válságállóbb lehet az idén, mint a világ
stories.summary: A közel-keleti konfliktus ezt az évet is hazavághatja a világgazdaságban, ugyanakkor a magyar gazdaságon, végre lendíthet az, ha a Tisza-kormány érdemi uniós forrást tud hazahozni.

stories.title: „Nyugi, nyugi, Xanax!” – a parlamenti napló szerint így szólogattak be eddig egymásnak a képviselők
stories.summary: A parlament gyorsírói azokat a bekiabálásokat is hallják és lejegyzik, amiket a tévénézők legfeljebb csak láthatnak. Az Országgyűlési Naplók alapján az első rendes ülésnapokon Magyar Péter volt a legaktívabb bekiabáló, de Pócs János, Dúró Dóra és Kocsis Máté sem hagyta magát. De ki érezte annyira fenyegetve magát, hogy az őrséget hívta?

stories.title: Leváltották a Nemzeti Infokommunikációs Szolgáltató Zrt. vezérigazgatóját
stories.summary: Tanács Zoltán miniszter Vetési Iván helyére Simon Lajost nevezte ki.

stories.title: Lithuania turns pink for giant cold soup festival
stories.summary: More than 100,000 people are expected to attend Vilnius’s Pink Soup Festival, a three-day celebration of Lithuania’s famous cold beet soup featuring races, costumes, foam slides and mass communal lunches.

stories.title: EU unblocks €16 billion in Hungary assistance as Magyar promises reforms
stories.summary: Reforms under new Prime Minister Peter Magyar will enable the EU to free up billions in frozen assistance for Budapest. The EU's Ursula von der Leyen announced the news as Magyar visited Brussels.

stories.title: Romania's political crisis deepens
stories.summary: After Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan’s government fell in early May, Romania's moderate parties have struggled to chart a new course — opening the door for the far right to gain ground.

stories.title: What Hungary must do to receive EU funds frozen under Orban
stories.summary: Prime Minister Peter Magyar and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen announced a deal that could free up over €16 billion in EU funds for Hungary. Now, Magyar's new government needs to deliver.

stories.title: Moment explosion erupts at Russian oil terminal in St. Petersburg after Ukrainian drone attack
stories.summary: Black smoke billowed over St. Petersburg after a Ukrainian drone strike sparked a fire at an oil terminal, according to Ukrainian and Russian officials. The attack came days before President Vladimir Putin is due to attend a major economic forum in the city.

stories.title: 2 researchers charged with smuggling mpox into the US
stories.summary: Federal law enforcement agencies on Tuesday charged two researchers at a National Institutes of Health lab in Montana with conspiracy to smuggle deactivated mpox virus into the United States and with giving false statements about it. The researchers are Vincent Munster, an award-winning scientist who heads the virus ecology section at the Rocky Mountain Laboratories in Hamilton, Montana, a small town an hour’s drive south of Missoula, and Claude Kwe, a research fellow in Munster’s section,

stories.title: Trump administration proposes 10 percent tariff on Canada, Mexico and the EU over forced labor
stories.summary: The Trump administration wants to reimpose a 10 percent tariff on top trading partners including the European Union and Canada, while hitting others with a higher rate, citing concerns about forced labor. The U.S. Trade Representative’s Office made those proposals as part of  a report it released late Tuesday  with the results of its investigation into 60 trading partners over their failure to impose and enforce laws to prohibit goods made with forced labor. It’s one of two sprawling t

stories.title: 4 ways Europe wants to wean off US tech
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Union wants to move away from foreign technology over concerns that overreliance has become a risk in shaky trade ties with Washington. The European Commission is finalizing a package of measures to boost its so-called technological sovereignty, set to be published on June 3. The package includes proposals to crack down on the bloc’s deep-rooted exposure to foreign technology in areas including cloud, artificial intelligence, microchips, software and data centers. “As geo

stories.title: Kuwait says airport hit by Iranian strikes as US and Iran launch fresh attacks
stories.summary: US military says it struck tanker and sites on Iran’s Qeshm Island and defended Kuwait and Bahrain from missiles Kuwait’s military said Iranian strikes that hit a terminal at its international airport caused significant damage and wounded several people. The attack came as the US and Iran exchanged fresh missile and drone strikes, further jeopardising efforts to secure a new ceasefire agreement between Washington and Tehran. Overnight, US forces fired a Hellfire missile to disable a tanker attem

stories.title: Midterm primaries 2026: results and reaction as six states including California and Iowa cast ballots – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. Follow our new live blog here Voters in Maine won’t head to the polls until next week, but the state’s primary election has been shaken up by recent news about Senate hopeful Graham Platner. According to information his wife shared with his campaign last year, Platner exchanged sexually explicit texts with other women during his marriage, the New York Times and the Wall Street Journal reported, and which the Guardian has confirmed. Graham Platner met on Tuesday with

stories.title: Ukraine strikes St Petersburg oil refinery – video
stories.summary: Ukrainian drones hit energy and military sites in St Petersburg early on Wednesday, according to footage shared by Volodymyr Zelenskyy on X. Several long-range drones crashed into oil storage facilities, as Russian air defences tried unsuccessfully to shoot them down. There were loud explosions and black smoke from the blazing oil terminal rose high above the city Ukraine strikes St Petersburg oil refinery Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump could slap Australia with 12.5% tariff for allegedly importing goods made by slave labour
stories.summary: Trade minister says Australia has ‘robust, comprehensive and world-leading legislation addressing forced labour and modern slavery’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia is among dozens of countries facing a 12.5% trade tariff from the Trump administration for allegedly failing to prevent imports of goods made by slave labour. The US trade representative, Jamieson Greer, listed Australia among 54 economies that “failed to impose and effectively enforce a prohibit

stories.title: Eleven officers and police dog injured in ‘terrifying’ violence in Southampton
stories.summary: Chief of Hampshire police says those claiming to protest over murder of Henry Nowak were ‘determined to spark fear and division’ UK politics live: latest news updates Eleven officers and a police dog were injured amid disorder by those claiming to protest over Henry Nowak’s murder, the chief of Hampshire police said, accusing those involved of being “determined to spark fear and division”. A trail of destruction was left on the streets of Southampton after Tuesday night’s riot with car windows s

stories.title: California primary unresolved as voters choose midterm candidates – US politics live
stories.summary: Democrats seek to oust Republicans in New Jersey and Iowa, while other major races remain up in the air US federal workers laid off by the Trump administration said they are experiencing mental health effects, including PTSD-like symptoms, from losing their jobs, according to a new survey. More than 300 fired probationary employees were surveyed, with 95% reporting ongoing mental health effects, according to 27UNIHTED, a network of former National Institute of Health (NIH) employees. Nearly half

stories.title: Trump threatens tariffs on 60 countries including UK and Canada over ‘forced labour’
stories.summary: Proposal for 10-12.5% levies, to also include EU, Taiwan, Canada and Australia, would allow US president to skirt court-imposed limits Donald Trump has threatened tariffs of between 10% and 12.5% on 60 countries including the UK, EU and Australia over alleged forced labour failures, in the latest attempt to revive his signature trade policy. The EU immediately hit back, saying it expected the US to respect the tariff deal it entered into last July and arguing that stealth tariffs breached the sp

stories.title: Portugal: General strike causes disruption to services
stories.summary: Flights have been canceled and schools closed as unions stage a strike over government labor reform plans. It is the second major walkout in Portugal in six months.

stories.title: Iranian drone attack causes heavy damage to Kuwait airport as US and Iran trade strikes
stories.summary: Kuwait suspended commercial flights after drones heavily damaged its international airport, hours after Iran fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain and the US struck an Iranian military facility on Qeshm Island. Iran's IRGC said it had targeted the US Navy's 5th Fleet headquarters in Bahrain.

stories.title: US and Iran launch new strikes, as Kuwait says airport hit by Iranian drones
stories.summary: Kuwait says one person is dead after drones hit civilian buildings including its international airport.

stories.title: California primary election results: governor’s race too close to call
stories.summary: Early results show Steve Hilton, Xavier Becerra and Tom Steyer emerging in front, with many ballots to be counted US politics live: latest news updates The high-stakes gubernatorial race in California remained too close to call on Tuesday night, with early results showing a tight contest in the crowded race. With many ballots still left to be counted, three candidates emerged at the top: the Republican Steve Hilton and the Democrats Xavier Becerra and Tom Steyer. And results were clear enough th

stories.title: Albanese calls Taylor ‘Temu Abbott’ as bitter fight rages over budget tax changes
stories.summary: Greens support needed to pass proposed changes but party concerned it will give government sweeping discretionary powers Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese has ridiculed Angus Taylor as a “Temu [Tony] Abbott” amid an increasingly bitter fight over tax changes in the budget, as the Greens raise concerns about key provisions of the government’s proposal. Labor will speed the first tranche of its budget legislation through the lower house on Thursday, and

stories.title: OECD says Australia should increase EV and renewable energy take up amid fuel crisis – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Chaney said: AI companies should be able to unlock the majority of global content with a handful of individual deals – in the same way that every other industry licenses copyrighted content. Government could further work to facilitate this process for the remainder of the copyrighted content, either directly or through a centralised mechanism. Continue reading...

stories.title: Far-right praise for shah’s secret police puts Reza Pahlavi on the spot
stories.summary: Son of deposed shah forced to distance himself from once-dreaded Savak as some of his ‘fascistic’ supporters glorify it For decades, the Savak was seen as the most hated symbol of repression that kept Iran’s last shah, Mohammad Reza Pahlavi, in power – and a main driving force behind the revolutionary fervor that toppled him in 1979. Now the deposed monarch’s son, Reza Pahlavi, has been forced to distance himself from the once-dreaded security agency after some of his most vociferous supporters

stories.title: Ukrainian drones hit St Petersburg as ‘Russian Davos’ opens in city
stories.summary: Energy and military sites targeted as guests gather for economic forum where Putin is due to speak on Friday Ukrainian drones hit energy and military sites in St Petersburg early on Wednesday hours before international guests gathered for the city’s flagship economic forum, in a blow to Vladimir Putin. Several long-range drones crashed into oil storage facilities after Russian air defences tried unsuccessfully to shoot them down. There were loud explosions and black smoke rose high above the cit

stories.title: Louisiana church solicits prayers for pastor convicted of sexual assault – but not for his victims
stories.summary: Anthony Odiong was sentenced to life in prison for sex crimes against his congregants in Texas A Louisiana church where a Roman Catholic priest served as pastor before being recently convicted in Texas of criminal clergy sexual assault and sentenced to life in prison has been criticized by his victims and their supporters for soliciting prayers on his behalf in a parochial bulletin published after his guilty verdict – which neglected to mention the survivors. One of the two women whom Anthony Od

stories.title: Starmer to face PMQs as police say 11 officers injured in disorder over Henry Nowak murder – UK politics live
stories.summary: PM to face questions from Kemi Badenoch and other MPs Zia Yusuf, the Reform UK home affairs spokesperson, told Sky News this morning that he thought the treatment of Henry Nowak did justify his party’s claim that two-tier policing operates in the country. He said: Having watched that footage [of Nowak’s arrest] … it’s hard to escape the conclusion that it is a demonstrable example of structural two-tier policing that is embedded in Hampshire police force and forces across the country. People can

stories.title: Hundreds of Russian strikes across Ukraine kill at least 14 people – video
stories.summary: Russia launched 73 missiles and 656 drones at Ukraine overnight, according to the Ukrainian air force, including eight hypersonic Tsirkon missiles. The main targets were Kyiv, Dnipro, Zaporizhzhia, Poltava and Kharkiv. The Ukrainian president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has asked his US counterpart, Donald Trump, to send Patriot missiles to the country after the Russian attacks killed at least 14 people and injured dozens more Zelenskyy asks Trump to send missiles after Russian strikes across Ukraine

stories.title: EU accused of creating ICE-style immigration enforcement system
stories.summary: Officials say law will improve migration management by allowing more deportations of undocumented people EU politicians have promised to increase deportations of undocumented migrants, under a new law that critics say mimics elements of the Trump administration’s brutal immigration crackdown. Finalising a key element of an overhauled EU asylum and migration system, politicians have agreed a regulation that will enable national authorities to raid people’s homes to enforce deportation orders. Con

stories.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon despite Trump’s effort to shore up ceasefire
stories.summary: Warplanes carry out dozens of airstrikes and Israeli army issues evacuation warning for city of Nabatiyeh Israeli warplanes have launched dozens of strikes across southern Lebanon despite a new agreement supposedly brokered by Donald Trump aiming to bolster the tattered ceasefire in Lebanon. The US president said on Monday that he had stopped an imminent Israeli strike on Beirut and that he had spoken to Israel’s prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, and representatives of Hezbollah and both agree

stories.title: Democrats who marched in New York’s Israel Day parade decry attendance of far-right minister Smotrich
stories.summary: Politicians’ statements reflect difficulty facing pro-Israel Democrats as voter support for country falls Several prominent New York Democrats who participated in the city’s annual Israel Day parade on Sunday have condemned the participation of Bezalel Smotrich, the far-right Israeli finance minister and a leading figure in the Israeli settler movement, in the event. Smotrich was among several Israeli lawmakers and cabinet officials who marched in the parade on Sunday. His appearance marked his

stories.title: Spain's Princess Leonor makes history with parachute training, reports say
stories.summary: Princess Leonor of Spain becomes the first Spanish royal to complete a parachuting course as part of her military training, according to Spanish media.

stories.title: UK: Protest erupts over student murder
stories.summary: Protesters have clashed with police in Southampton over the December murder of finance student Henry Nowak. The fatally injured victim was handcuffed by police after his killer accused him of racist abuse.

stories.title: Ukraine drones hit Saint Petersburg as economic forum starts
stories.summary: Ukraine has been stepping up long-range strikes as the war grinds on, with Russia's northerly second city now hit. The latest attack on Saint Petersburg takes place as an event long known as "Russia's Davos" kicks off.

stories.title: US and Iran exchange new strikes as ceasefire talks falter over Israel's military actions in Lebanon
stories.summary: Iran fired missiles toward Kuwait and Bahrain but failed to hit their targets, with two of those fired at Kuwait falling apart en route, while those aimed at Bahrain were intercepted, the US Central Command said.

stories.title: UK: Police and protesters clash at Southampton rally over student killing
stories.summary: Clashes broke out between protesters and police in Southampton after the release of bodycam footage linked to the fatal stabbing of student Henry Nowak sparked public anger.

stories.title: Seven killed after drone hits bus in Russia-controlled part of Ukraine
stories.summary: The bus was travelling between Moscow and Simferopol, in Russian-occupied Crimea, an official says.

stories.title: Republicans press Trump over controversial €1.5bn fund for victims of 'political weaponisation'
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump's Anti-Weaponisation Fund, launched to compensate individuals who believe they were subjected to politically motivated investigations, is being questioned by senators in his own party.

stories.title: South Africa police investigate killing of two Mozambican men
stories.summary: The deaths come after a day of violent protests against illegal migration in the town of Mossel Bay.

stories.title: Two arrested after four migrant farm workers killed in Italy minivan fire
stories.summary: CCTV images showed two people blocking the van's doors from the outside and throwing liquid inside to start a fire.

stories.title: Denmark’s Frederiksen unveils new left-leaning cabinet
stories.summary: Denmark’s caretaker Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen unveiled her third coalition government on Wednesday, putting an end to two months of political blockage following last March’s national election. After spending the past seven years governing with center-right parties, the Danish Social Democrat opted to form a left-leaning coalition with the insurgent Green Left party, the Social Liberals and The Moderates. Her cabinet’s 21 ministers include familiar faces like former prime

stories.title: UK will not have to pay Rwanda £100m over failed asylum scheme, court rules
stories.summary: Rwanda had sued UK government over alleged breach of agreement, after scheme scrapped by Labour on first day in office The UK will not have to pay the Rwandan government millions of pounds over a failed migrant deportation scheme set up by Boris Johnson’s administration, an international court has ruled. The east African nation had sued the current UK government for more than £100m, claiming it was owed after a breach of an agreement. Continue reading...

stories.title: Jim Chalmers is putting a positive spin on the economy, but is Australia’s outlook grim?
stories.summary: The economy was slowing even before the Middle East conflict and interest rate hikes started to bite, while the boom in datacentres was a rare bright spot Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The economy slowed sharply in early 2026 and Australians’ living standards are once again going backwards. At the risk of upsetting the treasurer by “talking down the economy”, as he put it, there wasn’t all that much to love in the latest national accounts. Continue reading...

stories.title: Italy votes to become a republic – archive, 1946
stories.summary: On 2 June 1946, in the first free election after the Mussolini dictatorship, Italy voted to abolish its monarchy and become a republic 6 June 1946 Continue reading...

stories.title: Nicotine use soars by 40% in eight years as Australian black market explodes
stories.summary: Australian Bureau of Statistics estimates nicotine consumption rose 40%, with illicit sources accounting for 80% of all tobacco Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australians are consuming more nicotine than they were eight years ago but are spending less, new data from the Australian Bureau of Statistics shows, as 80% of the cigarettes smoked by the nation last year were cheaper illegal products. The analysis relea

stories.title: Electrician guilty of murdering partner and blowing up their London home
stories.summary: Clifton George convicted of fatally stabbing charity worker Annabel Rook after she tried to end relationship A man who fatally stabbed his partner and then set off a gas explosion at their north-east London home last summer has been found guilty of her murder. Clifton George, 45, had denied murdering Annabel Rook, a 46-year-old charity founder, during an argument at their home in Stoke Newington, on 17 June 2025. He admitted her manslaughter. Continue reading...

stories.title: US primary voters choose midterm candidates as Democrats look to flip key seats
stories.summary: Democrats seek to oust Republicans in New Jersey and Iowa, while other major races remain up in the air The key race for California governor remained deadlocked as vote counting continued across the US on Wednesday morning following primary elections to decide who would run in several critical districts in the US House and Senate in November, in a midterm year expected to favor Democrats. In California, it was the Republican Steve Hilton, a former UK political operative and Fox News host, who ha

stories.title: Leftwing US pair refused entry to UK will address Oxford Union remotely
stories.summary: Home Office barred Cenk Uygur and Hasan Piker on grounds their visit was ‘not conducive to the public good’ Two leftwing US political commentators who were banned from entering the UK will still speak at the Oxford Union via livestream. The Home Office told Cenk Uygur and Hasan Piker their presence in the country was “not conducive to the public good” when they attempted to come to London to attend this week’s SXSW London event. Continue reading...

stories.title: Patriot missile shortage has created ‘window of vulnerability’ Russia is exploiting in Ukraine
stories.summary: Countries that rely on US-made air defence system feel increasingly exposed as interceptor supplies dwindle Russia is exploiting a critical global shortage of air defence interceptor missiles as it ramps up its airstrikes against Ukraine, amid warnings that a shortfall for the Patriot system in particular is creating a “window of vulnerability” for the countries that rely on them. The MIM-104 Patriot manufactured by Raytheon and Lockheed Martin is the primary surface-to-air system of the US mili

stories.title: Why Benjamin Netanyahu poses an obstacle to US and Iran peace deal
stories.summary: Prime minister under pressure to show his campaigns against Hamas, Hezbollah and Iran have brought results as he faces elections with his political survival at risk If there is to be a peace deal between the United States and Iran, it will have to go through a familiar obstacle: Benjamin Netanyahu. Israel’s military operations in Lebanon have become a sticking point in the talks for a potential opening of the strait of Hormuz – once again testing the volatile alliance between Donald Trump and Ne

stories.title: Iran war: US and Iran trade fire amid stalled talks
stories.summary: The US and Iran traded renewed fire in the Persian Gulf in a flareup of tensions on Wednesday. The fighting comes as peace efforts remain stalled.

stories.title: Democracy in Africa: Coups, crises and foregone conclusions
stories.summary: As Ethiopia formally counts the votes following Monday's election, attitudes toward democracy appear to be shifting across Africa. In April, Burkina Faso's junta leader told the Burkinabe people to "forget" democracy.

stories.title: US plans extra tariffs for 60 countries over forced labor
stories.summary: A new wave of Trump tariffs could unsettle US trade partners which have already signed new trade deals or have been negotiating them for months.

stories.title: India news: Several killed in New Delhi fire
stories.summary: At least 21 people were killed and several others injured after a fire tore through a building in New Delhi. Some of the victims were foreign nationals who had traveled to India for medical treatment. DW has more.

stories.title: Parliamentary elections in Armenia: What's at stake
stories.summary: Armenians are set to vote on the country's future geopolitical direction at the June 7 ballot. It will be a test of public opinion in Russia’s longstanding ally that has seen a gradual drift towards the EU.

stories.title: Who owns the billions in Germany's dormant bank accounts?
stories.summary: Billions of euros sit untouched in dormant bank accounts in Germany. With heirs unaware and no central register in place, banks and politicians are debating who should ultimately control the money.

stories.title: Germany news: Naturalizations reach record high in 2025
stories.summary: Naturalizations have increased for the fifth year in a row. Automotive industry firms turn a bit less pessimistic. And Germany is aiming for a seat on the UN Security Council. Follow DW for more news from Germany.

stories.title: Trump appoints Bill Pulte as acting intelligence chief
stories.summary: Pulte will take over the role of acting director of national intelligence after the departure of Tulsi Gabbard. He will be involved in important security matters as the US war with Iran continues.

stories.title: Putin remains uncompromising on Ukraine, but is public discourse on war changing in Russia?
stories.summary: Russia is intensifying attacks in Ukraine but more than four years of war are causing concern even among Putin loyalists.

stories.title: Sailors stressed and exhausted after months trapped by Strait of Hormuz blockade
stories.summary: The uncertainty has weighed heavily on the 20,000 seafarers trapped in the Iran war zone.

stories.title: Trump administration drops $1.8bn 'anti-weaponisation' fund
stories.summary: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche's announcement to abandon the fund comes amid pushback from top Republican lawmakers.

stories.title: Watch: Man attacked by bear at steel works in Japan
stories.summary: The bear, which injured four people in the attack on Tuesday, remains on the loose within the factory compound.

stories.title: Mysteriously missing congressman wins primary after Trump endorsement
stories.summary: Republican lawmaker Tom Kean Jr has not been seen in Washington or his New Jersey district for several months.

stories.title: Shell pumped oil through Nigeria pipeline for years despite pollution evidence, documents show
stories.summary: The oil giant says the documents ignore the critical context of the complex operating environment at the time.

stories.title: French artist JR's giant 'cave' installation in Paris appears damaged
stories.summary: Strong winds and heavy rain damaged artist JR’s giant “La Caverne” installation covering Paris’ Pont Neuf bridge, forcing organisers to postpone its public opening scheduled for June 6.

stories.title: British PM Starmer says Nigel Farage is attempting to 'create division' amid Henry Nowak protests
stories.summary: In a joint statement, a conglomerate of Sikh community groups condemned what it called a "moment of madness by an individual".

stories.title: A man in Turkey became one of the richest people in the world without realising it
stories.summary: With a balance of around $21.8 billion in his account, Takalo ranks among the world's super rich, but he has been unable to access his money for nearly a month.

stories.title: Foreign nationals among at least 21 killed in Delhi fire
stories.summary: Many victims were South Asians who had travelled to India for treatment or to accompany relatives.

stories.title: Two people shot dead in Kenya protests against Ebola plan
stories.summary: Kenyan police shot and killed two people who were protesting a US-backed plan to establish an Ebola quarantine facility in central Kenya.

stories.title: Italy marks 80 years of the Republic with military parade in Rome
stories.summary: Italy celebrated the 80th anniversary of the birth of the Republic with ceremonies, military parades and flypasts in Rome, commemorating the 1946 vote that paved the way for democracy.

stories.title: EU Commission set to slam Italy's fuel duty cuts as energy prices spike
stories.summary: The EU Commission is set to criticise the Italian government's reduction in excise duties on fuels in a report to be published on Wednesday. Rome has been asking for more fiscal flexibility to address the energy crisis, which Brussels deems should be directed at vulnerable families and industries.

stories.title: ‘You’re f****** crazy’: Trump clashes with Netanyahu as Lebanon tensions rise
stories.summary: Trump rebukes Benjamin Netanyahu over Lebanon escalation, warning strikes risk isolating Israel and derailing Iran talks, according to reports

stories.title: Arte: Just how corrupt is Europe?
stories.summary: With a former PM in Spain, an ex-president in France, and ministerial resignations in Ukraine, corruption appears to be rife across Europe.

stories.title: Parliament and Council disagree on air passenger rights but continue talks
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Co-legislators from the European Parliament and the Council of the EU paused negotiations on the air passenger rights reform on Wednesday morning without reaching a deal, but agreed to resume talks in the afternoon. Negotiations ended at 5 a.m. after 16 hours of inconclusive discussions on several parts of the reform , including the delay threshold granting the right to compensation and the level of compensation passengers would receive. Council and Parliament tried unsuccessfull

stories.title: UK competition watchdog forces Google to give publishers control over AI search
stories.summary: LONDON — The U.K.’s Competition and Markets Authority will force Google to give publishers greater control over how their content is used in AI-generated search results, it announced on Wednesday. Under the U.K.’s digital market regime, the CMA can impose “conduct requirements” on activities designated with “strategic market status.” Google search was designated with SMS status last year. The latest conduct requirement comes after Google said it plans to increasingly respond to search queries wi

stories.title: Dokument: Branche will 2026 knapp 11 Milliarden Euro in Netzausbau investieren
stories.summary: BERLIN – 2026 will die Telekommunikationsbranche 8,5 Milliarden Euro in den Glasfaserausbau und 2,4 Milliarden Euro in den Mobilfunk investieren. Dies geht aus der finalen Version eines Memorandum of Understanding hervor, das das Digitalministerium, die Länder, Kommunen und die Telekommunikationsbranche bei einem gemeinsamen Stakeholder-Dialog am 08. Juni unterzeichnen wollen. Das Dokument liegt POLITICO vor. Mit den Investitionen in den Glasfaserausbau sollen 3,2 Millionen Homes-Connected-Ansch

stories.title: Mexico City police teargas teachers’ protest 10 days before World Cup
stories.summary: Teachers associated with CNTE union were marching toward Zócalo for salary raises and reversal of pension laws Riot police fired teargas at teachers who were marching toward Mexico City’s historic Zócalo plaza, just days before the square is expected to host the 2026 World Cup “Fan Fest”. The incident is the second time police have clashed with teachers in the past week, and more conflict is likely as Mexico City prepares to hold the opening game of the Fifa World Cup on 11 June. “This event wil

stories.title: Mozambique says five citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa
stories.summary: South African police confirm two deaths of Mozambicans in Mossel Bay as anti-immigration violence sweeps country Mozambique said five of its nationals were killed in “xenophobic attacks” in South Africa at the weekend and efforts were under way on Tuesday to repatriate hundreds of others. However, the South African police confirmed only that two Mozambicans had died in violence in the southern coastal town of Mossel Bay, the first killings to be officially linked to a wave of anti-migrant protes

stories.title: UK wins court case over canceled plan to deport asylum seekers to Rwanda
stories.summary: An international court has rejected a Rwandan claim for millions in damages over a scrapped deal to send asylum seekers to the East African nation.

stories.title: UN urges calm as Israel advances into Lebanon and Iran threatens counter-fronts
stories.summary: The UN has called on all sides to respect the ceasefire following an Israeli military push into Lebanon, which threatens to derail US-Iran peace talks. Israel ordered strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs as Hezbollah targeted Haifa, prompting Iran to threaten the opening of new fronts.

stories.title: Massive overnight Russian missiles rock Ukraine, killing at least 18 people, authorities say
stories.summary: The latest attacks which have left at least 18 people dead and 116 injured, come days after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy warned that Russia was preparing a "massive new strike" on the country.

stories.title: Nightclub promoter, 21, stabbed to death after brawl in Dublin’s tourism district
stories.summary: Qayyum Balogun chased and attacked after gig ended in Grafton Street area following clash between rival groups, police say A brawl in the heart of Dublin’s tourism district led to a nightclub promoter being chased and stabbed to death. It happened at about 3am on Monday after a gig ended in the Grafton Street area of the city centre that is popular with tourists. Continue reading...

stories.title: Kyiv picks up the pieces after another attack by Russia – photo essay
stories.summary: On Monday night residents faced the third heavy assault on Ukraine’s capital in less than a month, as Putin appears to be trying to take advantage of a shortage of US-made air defence systems In the northern residential suburb of Vynohradar – a district of modest apartment blocks – residents were quietly and calmly getting on with salvaging, clearing and dealing with what remained of their apartments after Monday night’s massive missile attack on Kyiv. Dozens of rockets and hundreds of drones ha

stories.title: ‘We don’t have another country to run to’: Kenyans fear US plan for Ebola quarantine site
stories.summary: People from town of potential site for US citizens exposed to Ebola say it puts them at risk in country with no known cases People from a town in central Kenya where the US wants to set up an Ebola quarantine facility for its citizens have strongly criticised the plan, saying they fear it will expose them to the virus and that it is indicative of double standards on the part of the US. “Everybody should be quarantined in their home country. We shouldn’t allow foreigners to bring us diseases,” sa

stories.title: Unpacking Germany's campaign for a UN Security Council seat
stories.summary: On Wednesday, the United Nations will hold elections for several seats on the UN Security Council. Germany's government is campaigning vigorously for a seat.

stories.title: 'Cockroach' movement gives voice to India's angry youth
stories.summary: The satirical Cockroach Janta Party has tapped into the concerns of the India's youth over issues plaguing the nation's education and employment policies.

stories.title: Why Google wants to release millions of mosquitoes in the US
stories.summary: Google's Debug research program plans to release millions of sterile mosquitoes to fight species that spread diseases like dengue. How does the method work — and should humans interfere with nature like this?

stories.title: Kosovo general election: the cost of political polarization
stories.summary: Sunday's election in Kosovo — the third in under 16 months — is marked by a clash between former allies PM Albin Kurti and ex-President Vjosa Osmani, deadlock in the dialogue with Serbia and obstacles to EU integration.

stories.title: El Nino could push temperatures higher, UN warns
stories.summary: The World Meteorological Organization says there's a high likelihood of a moderate or possibly strong El Nino weather phenomenon in the coming months. This could push temperatures higher and affect rainfall.

stories.title: Germany: Cologne Cathedral to charge tourists €12 for entry
stories.summary: Cologne Cathedral has laid out plans to charge tourist visitors, starting in July. They will be asked to pay €12 each, to help cover maintenance costs. It still plans to keep a small free area available for worshippers.

stories.title: Bear injures four in residential area of Japan as annual number of attacks rises
stories.summary: Japan's Environment Ministry said 13 people were killed in more than 230 bear attacks in 2025, exceeding the number of fatalities and attacks in any preceding year.

stories.title: Giant Lionel Messi statue dismantled in Kolkata
stories.summary: A 21-metre statue of Lionel Messi in Kolkata has been removed less than a year after its unveiling due to safety fears over its stability in strong winds.

stories.title: Pashinyan moves to defuse Russia tensions while pledging Armenia's sovereignty ahead of crucial vote
stories.summary: With the latest polls showing him on course for a landslide victory on Sunday, PM Nikol Pashinyan told Armenians – and Russia – that he is seeking a balanced yet sovereign approach to his foreign policy, as Russia continued to exert pressure by imposing more sanctions ahead of the crucial vote.

stories.title: Cryptocurrency addiction is real — and it looks just like gambling
stories.summary: Jamie Giles is a therapist at Castle Craig, one of Scotland's leading addiction treatment centres. He spoke to Euronews about how to spot problematic crypto investing, who is most at risk and what to do about it.

stories.title: China's highest bridge attracts millions of visitors
stories.summary: China's Huajiang Grand Canyon Bridge has cut travel times, expanded 5G access and helped boost tourism and business in rural Guizhou.

stories.title: Half of Belgium sees the US as a bigger adversary than China
stories.summary: According to a recent poll, over 50 percent of Belgians view the US as an adversary. Meanwhile, only 40% to 44% perceive China as a rival.

stories.title: Maga influencer admits London Tube station assault
stories.summary: Melissa Rein Lively accepted a caution, while an assault by beating charge against her was withdrawn.

stories.title: Southern energy corridors set to dominate supplies to Europe, global leaders say
stories.summary: The rapid reshape of global energy supply routes and markets is a key focus at the Baku Energy Week 2026.

stories.title: British couple lose Iran jail sentence appeal, family says
stories.summary: Lindsay and Craig Foreman were arrested in January 2025 during a global motorbike journey.

stories.title: IRGC says Iran has more 'trump cards' up its sleeve as talks hang by a thread
stories.summary: A senior IRGC general said Iran had not revealed all its military capabilities and was ready for direct confrontation with the US and NATO, as hardliners linked to acting IRGC commander Ahmad Vahidi were identified as the force behind Monday's suspension of indirect negotiations with Washington.

stories.title: 'They'll fix the building, but not our souls': Sleepy Kyiv neighbourhood hit in Russian strike
stories.summary: Scenes of devastation greet residents emerging from underground shelters after a major Russian attack.

stories.title: Microsoft says new quantum chip 1,000 times more reliable than predecessor
stories.summary: The tech giant predicts it will have a quantum computer that can solve commercially useful problems by the end of the decade.

stories.title: Mayor cancels DR Congo friendly with Chile in Spain over Ebola concern
stories.summary: DR Congo's international friendly against Chile in Spain next week is cancelled after authorities raise health concerns over the Ebola outbreak in the African nation.

stories.title: Trump names housing official Bill Pulte as acting US spymaster
stories.summary: The head of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, who pushed for criminal charges against Trump's foes, will step in as acting Director of National Intelligence.

stories.title: Massive drug tunnel uncovered beneath US-Mexico border
stories.summary: US authorities released footage of a sophisticated tunnel running beneath the US-Mexico border, saying it was used to smuggle more than $45 million worth of cocaine from Tijuana into California.

stories.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon but partial truce with Hezbollah appears to hold
stories.summary: Israel continues its campaign in the south, though has not struck Beirut after a deal announced by the US.

stories.title: Az itáliai hadvezér, akinek a címerét három herezacskó díszíti
stories.summary: Bartolomeo Colleoni neve pechjére nagyon hasonlít az olasz here szóra, de ez a legkevésbé sem zavarta, sőt a család címerén is büszkén viselte élete végéig.

stories.title: Ezért nem jó ötlet a polgármesteri béreken spórolni, hiába hangzik jól
stories.summary: Egy friss tanulmány szerint a magasabb politikusi bérek csökkentik a közbeszerzési korrupciós kockázatot az önkormányzatoknál.

stories.title: Arte: Vajon megfékezhető-e a brüsszeli intézményeket is elérő korrupció?
stories.summary: Bár globálisan Európa a leginkább korrupciómentes régió, a politikai botrányok a legmagasabb szinteket is megrengetik.

stories.title: Kratos halott felesége egy beszélő zselékocka oldalán dúlja fel az istenek túlvilágát az új God of Warban
stories.summary: A Sony idei stratégiája a jelek szerint az, hogy minden bőrt le kell húzni a God of Warról, és ha tényleg ez a cél, akkor jól is csinálják. De azért másra is jutott idő a keddi State of Playen.

stories.title: Erősebb szél, sok csapadék és apró jég – egy hidegfront miatt lesz zivataros a szerda
stories.summary: Van térség, ahol felhőszakadás is kialakulhat napközben.

stories.title: Visszakozott a Trump-kormányzat a republikánusokat is felháborító pénzalap ügyében
stories.summary: Mégsem hozzák létre azt az 1,8 milliárd dolláros alapot, amiből az elnök támogatóit fizették volna ki.

stories.title: Az Integritás Hatóság szerint a túlárazott atlétikai vb ügye mutatja, miért van rájuk szükség
stories.summary: Szerintük ez az eset pontosan megmutatja, mire jó egy antikorrupciós hatóság.

stories.title: Irán és az Egyesült Államok újra lőni kezdte egymást
stories.summary: Az iráni állami média szerint napok óta leálltak a béketárgyalások, Donald Trump szerint azonban folyamatosan beszélnek egymással.

stories.title: Máris leminősítették azt a céget, amit a Tiborcz-kör méregdrágán adott el a tőzsdén jegyzett Alteónak
stories.summary: A német Scope ratingcég szerint a gyenge cash flow, a romló tőkehelyzet miatt a cég egyre jobban a tulajdonos támogatására szorulhat.

stories.title: Zelenszkij: Olajterminált támadtak ukrán drónok Szentpéterváron
stories.summary: A támadás a Vlagyimir Putyin orosz elnök által tartott nemzetközi gazdasági fórum nyitónapján történt.

stories.title: Az idő Iránnak segít, kritikus szint alá csökkenhet a globális olajkészlet
stories.summary: A világgazdaság normál működtetéséhez mintegy 6,8 milliárd hordó olajtartalék szükséges, jelenleg már csak 7,5 milliárd hordó áll rendelkezésre, és minden nap apad a készlet.

stories.title: Orosz hatóságok: Ukrán dróntámadás ért egy buszt, heten meghaltak
stories.summary: A támadásban 11-en megsebesültek. Az orosz hatóságok terrortámadásként kezelik az esetet.

stories.title: Meghalt a székesfehérvári házomlás sérültje
stories.summary: A négyemeletes ipari épület bontás közben omlott össze, a sérültet súlyos-életveszélyes állapotban mentették ki a romok alól.

stories.title: A KDNP-s Máthé Zsuzsa szerint a szívhangrendelet eltörlésével magukra hagynák a krízishelyzetbe került nőket
stories.summary: A kereszténydemokrata képviselő szerint fontos tudatosítani a döntés előtt álló nőkben, hogy „egy másik ember szíve pulzál az ő szívük alatt”.

stories.title: Hamarosan megtudhatjuk, milyen műtárgyakat adtak kölcsön a közgyűjteményekből a korábbi kormányok
stories.summary: A Sári Zsolt közgyűjteményi és örökségvédelemért felelős államtitkár által bejelentett intézkedés a műtárgyak és kulturális javak digitalizációs programjához is szorosan kapcsolódni fog.

stories.title: A rák, ami egyszerűen csak van
stories.summary: Urfi Péter Sansz című könyve a koronavírus-járvány krónikájának indult, de beleszövődött egy agydaganat története. Szépirodalom, hírek, mese, vers, dal keveredik orvostudománnyal és nagyon hétköznapi dolgokkal.

stories.title: Kapitány István annyira rápörgött a kávéra, hogy azt is kiszámolta, mennyi jönne ki az EU-s forrásokból
stories.summary: „Az nem gyenge”, állapítja meg a lassan a kávéért felelős miniszteri címet is kiérdemlő miniszter.

stories.title: Gyurkó Szilvia: 90 százalékig biztos, hogy a javítóintézetek visszakerülhetnek a gyermekvédelembe
stories.summary: A javítóintézetek irányítása és fenntartása tavaly decemberben a Büntetés-végrehajtás Országos Parancsnokságához került.

stories.title: Új, illetékmentes számlacsomaggal is spórolhatnak a kisvállalkozások
stories.summary: Olyan bankszámla akció jelent meg június elején, aminek láttán azt hihetik a vállalkozások, hogy megszüntették az átutalásaikat terhelő vaskos tranzakciós illetéket. Az új ajánlat viszont csak néhány hónapig elérhető induló vállalkozásoknak és egyéni vállalkozóknak. A BiztosDöntés.hu összeállításából az is kiderül, hogy a kisvállalkozások még milyen számlacsomagokkal tudják olcsón megúszni a mindennapi bankolásukat.

stories.title: Reagált a Fidesz az óbudai korrupciós ügyre: Mindenkinek vállalnia kell a felelősséget tetteiért
stories.summary: A két éve indult, és eddig csak az óbudai önkormányzatot és annak cégeit érintő ügy nyomozása a héten már két másik baloldali vezetésű önkormányzatra is kiterjedt.

stories.title: Szoboszlai Dominik: A divatban semmi sem túlzás
stories.summary: A focista legutóbbi szettje mémesedett, de ez a jelek szerint egyáltalán nem zavarja.

stories.title: Gázolt a HÉV Óbudán
stories.summary: A szerelvények egy pályán közlekednek Szentendre és Békásmegyer között.

stories.title: Leváltották az Origo főszerkesztőjét
stories.summary: Pósa Árpádot a Bors Online főszerkesztője, Végh László váltja megbízott főszerkesztőként.

stories.title: „Mi nem teljesítettük” – ismerte el Navracsics egy vagyonnyilatkozati elvárásról az EU-pénzekért
stories.summary: A vagyonnyilatkozatok ellenőrzésénél parlamentarizmushoz kötődő érvek voltak, hogy a képviselőkről a mentelmi bizottság ítélkezhessen, közölte a volt miniszter.

stories.title: Lőrincz Viktória szorosabb együttműködést ígért az önkormányzatoknak
stories.summary: A vidék- és településfejlesztési miniszter bemutatkozott az önkormányzati érdekképviseletek vezetőinek.

stories.title: Milánóban nyoma veszett két mozaikherének
stories.summary: Eddig egy 19. századi bevásárlóárkád padlóján található, nemrég restaurált bikához tartoztak, és követeli őket vissza a népharag.

stories.title: Trump admirer’s surprise first-round win is a blow to Colombia’s traditional conservatives
stories.summary: Success of far-right presidential candidate, Abelardo de la Espriella, suggests some voters are ‘fed up with politics’ The far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella and the leftwing senator Iván Cepeda have just under three weeks to compete for the roughly 3.6m votes that did not go to either of them in the first round of Colombia’s presidential election. That is no insignificant number, given that De la Espriella’s lead over Cepeda amounted to little more than 670,000 votes – 43.7% to 40.9%. Co

stories.title: Zelenskyy asks Trump to send missiles after Russian strikes across Ukraine
stories.summary: At least 18 killed, dozens injured and others trapped under collapsed buildings after attacks on five Ukrainian cities Volodymyr Zelenskyy has asked Donald Trump to send Patriot missiles to Ukraine after a devastating Russian attack killed at least 18 people and injured dozens more. Russia launched 73 missiles and 656 drones at Ukraine overnight, according to the air force, including eight hypersonic Tsirkon missiles. The main targets were Kyiv, the central cities of Dnipro and Zaporizhzhia, and

stories.title: Refugees and migration: Is Europe closing its doors?
stories.summary: Migration researchers have called on European governments to abandon isolationist policies. But many politicians are not heeding their calls.

stories.title: French Navy boards Russia-linked tanker in Atlantic Ocean
stories.summary: Video released by France shows naval forces boarding the Russia-linked tanker Tagor in the Atlantic Ocean. Paris says the vessel was intercepted as part of efforts to enforce sanctions and curb Russian oil exports funding the war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Kenya protesters oppose Ebola quarantine centre
stories.summary: Hundreds protested in Nanyuki against a planned Ebola quarantine centre as Kenya's High Court suspended the project pending review.

stories.title: Tehran suspended negotiations via mediators with US, Iranian media says
stories.summary: Iran has suspended all indirect talks with the US and announced it would pursue "complete closure of Strait of Hormuz," accusing Israel of "continuing crimes" in Lebanon and stating that any ceasefire breach on one front breaks the truce in general.

stories.title: Driver with no right hand ticketed for using phone in her right hand
stories.summary: Bodycam video from Florida shows a police officer issuing a texting-while-driving citation to a woman, despite her not having a right hand. The ticket has since been recommended for dismissal, according to US media reports.

stories.title: Moment bear chases and attacks workers in Japan
stories.summary: CCTV footage captured a bear chasing and attacking a worker outside a factory in Fukushima. Four people were injured as the animal wandered through a residential area, prompting a large-scale response by police and emergency services.

stories.title: People ‘panicking’ as Ghana passes sweeping law criminalising LGBTQ+ activity
stories.summary: Community groups say some fear they could lose homes, jobs and access to healthcare if the new law is ratified by President John Dramani Mahama Ghana’s LGBTQ community is living in fear after the country’s parliament approved a sweeping bill that criminalises the promotion of LGBTQ+ activities and identifying as lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender or queer, rights groups have warned. The legislation, which was passed on Friday, mandates prison sentences of three to 10 years. Continue reading...

stories.title: EasyJet is an obvious takeover target, but US approach may not be a flyer | Nils Pratley
stories.summary: Castlelake’s move raises questions over valuation and ownership rules as well as whether Stelios Haji-Ioannou could throw a spanner in the works EasyJet says US takeover bid would be ‘highly opportunistic’ A share price gain of only 10% on a possible takeover approach is a meek reaction. If the stock market truly believed that Castlelake, a US investment fund, stood a decent chance of buying easyJet , you would expect the target’s stock to fly significantly higher. Scepticism is the right stance

stories.title: Musket balls and a burnt hull: evidence of real pirates of the Caribbean found in Bahamas
stories.summary: Exclusive: First shipwrecks found in Nassau harbour on New Providence, once the hideout of Blackbeard and Calico Jack The first shipwrecks linked to the real pirates of the Caribbean in the Bahamas have been discovered by an international team co-directed by a British marine archaeologist. Blackbeard and Calico Jack Rackham were among pirates who, between the 1690s and 1720s, turned Nassau on the island of New Providence into a hideout where they plotted their next heists on the high seas and di

stories.title: Denmark’s Mette Frederiksen to lead four-party coalition after months of uncertainty
stories.summary: Social Democrat stays on for third consecutive term as PM and will outline key policies by the end of Tuesday Denmark’s new government is preparing to formally present its political programme after Mette Frederiksen negotiated a third consecutive term as prime minister, this time at the head of a four-party, left-leaning minority coalition. Announced late on Monday, the agreement between Frederiksen’s Social Democrats, the Social Liberals, the Green Left and the centrist Moderates ended two mont

stories.title: Az utolsó Eufória furán üt be
stories.summary: Az Eufória harmadik évadának az a titka, hogy nem Eufóriaként kell nézni. Ha sikerül elvonatkoztatni attól, amit ismerünk és szeretünk, akkor kapunk egy egész jó gengsztersztorit, ami egy hosszú hétvégén ledarálható. Kritika.

stories.title: Sikerrel vette az első akadályt az EU és az USA vámalkuja az Európai Parlamentben
stories.summary: Egy fideszes képviselő szerint az egyezségen nem sikerült érdemben javítani, de az elutasításával „egy nyílt kereskedelmi háborút kockáztatnánk”.

stories.title: Bírósági ügye állhat a háttérben, hogy visszavonták egy vb-re induló svájci focista belépését Amerikába
stories.summary: Breel Embolo az utolsó pillanatban nem tudott elutazni a foci-vb-re Kaliforniába, még vizsgálják az ügyét.

stories.title: Szeptember 30-ig le kell bontani a gigaplakátokat a most megjelent törvényjavaslat szerint
stories.summary: A többi reklámeszköz tulajdonosainak is jelentkezniük kell az illetékes önkormányzatnál eddig a határidőig, hogy megvizsgálják, megfelel-e a hirdetésük az új követelményeknek.

stories.title: Megjelent a törvényjavaslat, amivel a Tisza Párt megszüntetné a Szuverenitásvédelmi Hivatalt
stories.summary: A tiszás Melléthei-Barna Márton kedd késő este nyújtotta be az erről szóló törvényjavaslat-csomagot.

stories.title: Ethiopia goes electric following gas car ban
stories.summary: Electric vehicles have taken off in Ethiopia. Key to the shift: a world-first ban on importing fossil fuel-powered vehicles.

stories.title: Legendary warrior women: The history of the Amazons
stories.summary: They're said to have cut off their breasts to better draw their bows, and killed their male offspring. What's true behind the myths surrounding the Amazons?

stories.title: Watch: Glass doors break as fans scramble to see Pursuit of Jade star
stories.summary: Huge crowds formed outside a shopping centre in China to see actor Zhang Linghe.

stories.title: Kyrgyzstan calls for more Asian, African and Latin American seats on UN Security Council
stories.summary: As he announced his country's candidacy for a non-permanent seat on the UN Security Council, Kyrgyzstan's Foreign Minister Jeenbek Kulubaev called for its reform to include greater representation from Asia, Africa and South America.

stories.title: Trump says he secured halt to Israel’s offensive in Lebanon, keeps door open for Iran talks
stories.summary: The US president further eased tensions sparked by Tehran's announcement on Monday of an end to mediated negotiations, saying in a separate post on Truth Social that talks with the Islamic Republic continued "at a rapid pace".

stories.title: ‘I felt a vibration’: Resident describes powerful meteor explosion over Massachusetts
stories.summary: A Massachusetts resident described feeling her apartment shake after a meteor exploded high above New England, releasing energy equivalent to about 300 tons of TNT, according to NASA.

stories.title: 'Mornings and nights no longer exist' at 47C: A day in the hottest place in India
stories.summary: What a day in India's hottest district reveals about life on the frontline of extreme heat.

stories.title: US to slash number of embassies in Africa processing visas
stories.summary: Currently there are around 50 US embassies and consulates processing visa applications in Africa, and that number is expected to be significantly reduced to 20 in the coming weeks, according to reports.

stories.title: From Almaty to the skies: Kazakhstan tests Central Asia's first air taxi
stories.summary: Central Asia's first air taxi test flight has taken place in Kazakhstan, as authorities and private partners work to develop a new urban air mobility network. Commercial trial operations are expected by early 2029, subject to certification and regulatory approval.

stories.title: Kyiv residents shelter underground during Russian attack
stories.summary: Residents took refuge in Kyiv’s metro system as Russian missile and drone attacks hit the Ukrainian capital overnight, causing fires, damaging buildings and injuring several people.

stories.title: Pentagon bans journalists from press office, citing classified material
stories.summary: The Pentagon has barred journalists from entering its press office after redesignating the space as a Sensitive Compartmented Information Facility (SCIF), a secure area used for handling classified information.

stories.title: Kazakhstan open to hosting Iran's uranium stockpile if nuclear deal is reached
stories.summary: Kazakhstan has offered to store Iran’s near-weapons-grade uranium to break the deadlock in US–Iran talks, as IAEA chief Grossi backed the neutral plan.

stories.title: Watch: Explosion at fireworks factory in Malta
stories.summary: Two men were injured in the explosion and were taken to hospital with minor injuries.

stories.title: Seven killed in Iowa shooting, including gunman
stories.summary: A shooting across several locations in Muscatine, Iowa, has left seven people dead, including the gunman.

stories.title: Israel and Hezbollah exchange fire despite Trump announcing breakthrough agreement
stories.summary: Israel and Hezbollah exchange fire after Donald Trump announced a halt, casting doubt on ceasefire efforts.

stories.title: He was a reality show villain. Can he be the mayor of one of America's largest cities?
stories.summary: Republican Spencer Pratt, known for his TV role on The Hills, appears to be in a tight race with two Democrats in Los Angeles.

stories.title: Clashes in Paris after PSG win Champions League final – video
stories.summary: French police clashed with rioters in the capital after Paris Saint-Germain defeated Arsenal to win the Champions League title . Hundreds of people were detained across France, with the majority in the Paris area French police arrest 780 after violent clashes as PSG fans celebrate Champions League win Continue reading...

stories.title: Iran executes two men over alleged mosque attack as crackdown deepens
stories.summary: Two men were executed after being convicted of setting a Tehran mosque on fire during protests that erupted over the collapse of the rial, as Amnesty International documented 39 political executions and more than 6,000 arrests since the war began in February.

stories.title: Wildfires devastating richer areas but fewer hectares burned globally – study
stories.summary: ‘Megafires’ in California, Canada, South Korea and Europe in 2025, but changes to farming slowed spread in parts of Africa “Devastating” wildfires ripped across the wealthier parts of the world in 2025, a study has found, even as globally, the area ravaged by flames fell. Catastrophic blazes claimed lives, homes and jobs last year in California, Canada, Europe and South Korea. But the 335m hectares burned was the second-lowest since 2002, the review found, largely owing to the expansion of Afric

stories.title: Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters
stories.summary: Powerful winds and rain expected in parts of Japan and Australia, while temperatures in Spain could hit 40C A powerful tropical storm is forecast to track near Okinawa, Japan, on Monday before moving towards the south-east of the country. Typhoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) has formed within the monsoonal gyre over the Philippine Sea. A monsoonal gyre is a large, slow-rotating weather system that spawns typhoons through smaller vortices formed within it. This flow can intensify storms. S

stories.title: Space race: Why Portugal is reaching for the stars
stories.summary: A spaceport is being built on a small Portuguese island in the Atlantic. Is Portugal on the verge of becoming a space nation?

stories.title: How the Alevi community came to thrive in Germany
stories.summary: The Alevis make up the fourth largest religious community in Germany today. What are the main tenets of their faith? And how are they preserving their unique culture?

stories.title: Germany news: Most expect AfD state premier after elections
stories.summary: Most people in Germany expect a premier from the far-right AfD to lead at least one state after elections in the autumn, a survey has found. The Brenner Pass linking Austria and Italy has reopened.

stories.title: DNA had one rule. Bacteria didn't get the memo
stories.summary: Every cell in every organism on Earth copies DNA the same way. Except one bacterial protein — quietly doing something scientists had never seen before.

stories.title: The German town that prints its own money to cut emissions
stories.summary: It started as a school project to promote local businesses in a remote Bavarian region. Now, the "Chiemgauer" currency helps to cut emissions.

stories.title: Young German talents impress in World Cup tune-up vs Finland
stories.summary: Deniz Undav and Lennart Karl delivered strong performances in Germany's last game at home before the start of 2026 FIFA World Cup.

stories.title: How China keeps North Korea's economy alive
stories.summary: Pyongyang relies on China for 95% of its legitimate trade; hacking, arms deals and remittances also help it stay afloat.

stories.title: Between celebration and confrontation: Paris after PSG victory
stories.summary: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police following the Champions League final.

stories.title: Marilyn Monroe 'lookalikes' gather to celebrate her 100th birthday
stories.summary: Fans set a new world record for the largest gathering of people dressed as Marilyn Monroe to celebrate what would have been the legendary actress’ centenary.
